name: Lemon Chicken #3
recipeInstructions:
1. Preparation: Pull the chicken skin from the breasts, then carefully pull the meat from the breast bone, keeping the meat intact. The thin inner filet will come off separately. Cut the meat across the grain into strips about 3/4 inch wide and 1 1/2 inches long.
2. Coat in powder: Put water chestnut powder in a bowl large enough to hold chicken and deep enough to keep powder from flying around. Add chicken pieces a few at a time, tossing gently to thoroughly coat each piece; they should be entirely white. Leave them in the bowl while you prepare the sauce.
3. Prepare sauce: In a small saucepan, mix prepared lemon sauce (we suggest Mee Chun brand from Hong Kong), water, and sugar. Bring to boil; reduce heat and simmer for 2 minutes. Just before serving, thicken with cornstarch paste to the consistency of a fudge sauce.
4. Deep-frying: In a deep-fryer or wok, slowly heat oil to deep-frying temperature (do not let oil smoke). Test by frying a piece of chicken; it should reach medium brown in about 35 seconds. Fry chicken in batches of 5-6 pieces at a time and drain. Place on serving plate, keeping warm until ready to serve. Pour lemon sauce over chicken just before serving.
